evals.prompt: We already have a docs page about service boundaries, but the frontmatter is messy and the docs reader keeps struggling to tell if it is current or what it depends on. Update the doc so the metadata clearly explains what the document is, who it is for, and which docs it depends on. Keep the body edits minimal unless the frontmatter implies a lifecycle change.
evals.expected_output: Routes to Maintain mode and updates an existing doc by prioritizing frontmatter clarity, lifecycle fields, and dependency metadata instead of rewriting prose.
assertions: Routes to existing-doc maintenance before reclassification.
Adds or fixes universal frontmatter fields from contracts/frontmatter.md.
Preserves body content unless a lifecycle field requires a body update.
Uses templates/reports/mutation-report.md.

evals.prompt: We accepted an ADR last quarter, but production load testing showed the full service mesh adds unacceptable latency. Create the follow-up ADR that replaces it with gateway policy plus service libraries for retries and mTLS, make the supersession relationship explicit, and avoid editing the accepted ADR in place.
evals.expected_output: Creates a new ADR with lifecycle handling and supersession metadata, rather than mutating the accepted ADR body or treating the old ADR as a current dependency.
assertions: Does not edit the accepted ADR body except Status Log.
Sets old ADR status to superseded and populates supersededBy with an ADR ID.
Creates a new ADR using templates/authoring/adr.md shape.
Uses supersedes with an ADR ID, not a path.
Does not add the superseded ADR as a current depends-on dependency.

evals.prompt: Our rollout plan for the docs revamp has two milestones done and one overdue. Update the plan document status, refresh its review metadata, and identify which linked docs should be revisited because the plan changed.
evals.expected_output: Treats the plan as a living doc: updates status and review metadata, preserves deliverable-oriented milestones, and uses cascade metadata to point at impacted docs.
assertions: Keeps the doc classified as type: explanation with kind including plan.
Updates status, lastReviewed, and Status Log for the plan.
Identifies docs in updates or cascade output that need review.
Uses templates/reports/mutation-report.md.

evals.prompt: A docs audit found that the service-boundaries overview depends on the API ownership doc, but the frontmatter points to an old path and the reverse cascade is missing. Repair the depends-on/updates chain so read and audit workflows can trace the relationship cleanly.
evals.expected_output: Repairs existing cascade metadata instead of creating a new doc: fixes the stale depends-on path, adds the inverse updates entry where appropriate, runs or references cascade tracing, and reports only changed metadata and followups.
assertions: Routes to Maintain mode and cascade repair.
Fixes stale depends-on path without rewriting unrelated frontmatter.
Adds or recommends inverse updates metadata for the referenced doc.
Runs or explicitly invokes scripts/cascade_targets.py for validation.

evals.prompt: Read the service mesh ADR and tell me whether it is still current before summarizing it. If it was superseded, follow the replacement and summarize the current decision instead.
evals.expected_output: Routes to Read mode, skips the superseded ADR as current guidance, follows supersededBy, and separates historical context from current guidance.
assertions: Reads frontmatter before body content.
Does not summarize a superseded ADR as current guidance.
Follows supersededBy when present.
Uses templates/reports/read-status.md.

evals.prompt: Run a docs health audit focused on dependency chains. Some docs still depend on an ADR that has been superseded. Report the broken chain and recommend the exact replacement link.
evals.expected_output: Routes to Audit mode, flags depends-on entries pointing to superseded docs, recommends replacement through supersededBy or stable spec, and does not confuse ADR lineage with current dependency.
assertions: Flags depends-on entries pointing to superseded docs.
Uses supersededBy to recommend a replacement when available.
Distinguishes broken current dependency from historical ADR supersession lineage.
Uses templates/reports/health-report.md.

evals.prompt: Update the API ownership reference and then make sure every downstream doc that relies on it can be traced by both read and audit workflows.
evals.expected_output: Routes to Maintain mode, updates the reference metadata, traces downstream docs, repairs inverse depends-on/updates links when safe, and reports one-way exceptions explicitly.
assertions: Updates lastReviewed on the changed doc.
Identifies downstream docs through updates and incoming depends-on links.
Repairs missing inverse depends-on or updates metadata when safe.
Runs or explicitly invokes cascade tracing.
Reports changed files and followups compactly.

evals.prompt: Read the TIL about cascade repair and tell me whether it is canonical guidance or just a practical note. Use its metadata and body together before answering.
evals.expected_output: Routes to Read mode, recognizes kind: [til] as a practical note, and avoids promoting it into durable canonical guidance.
assertions: Treats kind: [til] as valid metadata.
Describes the doc as a practical note rather than a current spec.
Uses linked spec for canonical current behavior when needed.
Does not recommend reclassifying the TIL as a spec or reference.

evals.prompt: Audit the service boundary contract frontmatter and tell me whether its classification is current. Be careful not to discard any valid secondary kind values.
evals.expected_output: Routes to targeted Audit or Read mode, preserves kind: [spec, ssot] as valid, and treats the doc as the canonical current dependency target.
assertions: Accepts multiple kind values when both are true.
Does not normalize kind: [spec, ssot] down to [spec].
Treats the document as canonical current truth.
Does not report list syntax for kind as invalid.

evals.prompt: Create a rollout plan for migrating docs authors to the new unified-docs lifecycle. It should be milestone-oriented, not a tiny task checklist, and it should be easy to audit for stale status later.
evals.expected_output: Routes to Create mode, uses the plan authoring skeleton, includes reviewCadence: 90, depends-on and updates lists, and structures the body around goal, scope, milestones, risks, and status log.
assertions: Uses templates/authoring/plan.md structure.
Sets type: explanation and kind including plan.
Includes reviewCadence: 90 by default.
Includes depends-on and updates even if empty.
Uses deliverable-oriented milestones rather than task crumbs.

evals.prompt: The docs cleanup plan is complete. Create the durable spec that should become current truth, and make sure the completed plan is not treated as the current dependency target anymore.
evals.expected_output: Routes to Create/Maintain lifecycle handling, creates a spec-shaped current truth doc, and treats the completed plan as historical once durable outcomes move into the spec.
assertions: Uses templates/authoring/spec.md structure.
Sets type: reference and kind including spec.
Includes reviewCadence: 180 by default.
Does not make current docs depend on an archived or completed plan as current truth.
Reports lifecycle followups compactly.

evals.prompt: Turn this incident note into a TIL: we learned that cascade links need inverse metadata, but the durable rule should live in the lifecycle spec. Make the distinction clear.
evals.expected_output: Routes to Create mode, uses the TIL authoring skeleton, marks the doc as practical note, and links durable behavior to a spec instead of making the TIL canonical.
assertions: Uses templates/authoring/til.md structure.
Sets kind: [til].
Does not add reviewCadence by default.
Includes depends-on and updates lists.
States that the TIL is not canonical specification.
